description: Topic queue for Geo-Instructor blog auto-generation. The cron job pops the first 'pending' topic, generates a post, validates it with 3 reader subagents, and if approved marks it 'published'. If rejected, marks it 'revision_needed' with feedback.

topics.title: Signed Distance Fields — The Shape of Nothing
topics.description: Every point in space knows how far it is from the nearest surface. This simple idea unlocks CSG modeling, real-time ray marching, collision detection, and neural implicit surfaces.

topics.title: Delaunay & Voronoi — The Dual Dance
topics.description: Two structures. One set of points. Delaunay connects them into nice triangles. Voronoi carves space into territories. Together they solve problems from mesh generation to nearest-neighbor search.

topics.title: Marching Cubes — Building Surfaces from Fog
topics.description: How do you turn a 3D grid of density values into a triangle mesh? Walk through each cube, look up a table, and connect the dots. The most-cited algorithm in scientific visualization.

topics.title: The Half-Edge — A Data Structure for Meshes
topics.description: Every mesh operation — subdivision, simplification, traversal — depends on answering one question fast: who are my neighbors? The half-edge data structure is the answer.

topics.title: Bézier Curves — Drawing with Control Points
topics.description: Four points define a curve. Move any point and the curve follows smoothly. De Casteljau's algorithm reveals why, one linear interpolation at a time.

topics.title: Spatial Hashing — Finding Neighbors in Constant Time
topics.description: Forget tree traversals. Hash particle positions into grid cells and check only neighbors. The simplest spatial data structure, and often the fastest for uniform distributions.

topics.title: Verlet Integration — Simulating without Velocity
topics.description: Store two positions instead of position + velocity. Constraints become trivial. The backbone of every real-time physics engine from Hitman to Vellum.

topics.title: Ray-Triangle Intersection — The Atom of Ray Tracing
topics.description: Every ray tracer, every physics engine, every collision detector calls this routine billions of times. Barycentric coordinates make it elegant.
